environment.supplementaryLocalChecks: macOS arm64, .NET SDK 10.0.301, EnableWindowsTargeting=true; local test execution not possible because the WPF test assembly requires the Windows Desktop runtime

validatedActions.details: Central package management (Directory.Packages.props) with committed per-project packages.lock.json; locked mode succeeded against the committed lock graph.

validatedActions.action: Release build and static analysis on Windows
validatedActions.details: 0 warnings, 0 errors. .NET analyzers enabled (AnalysisLevel latest); warnings treated as errors in CI. WPF application and test assemblies produced.

Coverage: project structure, deterministic-restore inputs, CI workflow presence, dependency registration including absence of later-phase service registrations, configuration loading and version-1 validation rejection, prohibited authentication/API patterns, no employee-password surface, SQLite schema foundation creation and idempotency, and a Windows-only WPF window construction smoke test.

No vulnerable packages detected. NuGet audit (NU1901-NU1904) is enforced as errors on every restore; the transitive SQLitePCLRaw 2.1.11 advisory GHSA-2m69-gcr7-jv3q was remediated by pinning SQLitePCLRaw.bundle_e_sqlite3 2.1.12.

27 source and build files scanned for 15 prohibited patterns covering Graph beta, application permissions, write permissions, resource-owner password credentials, device-code flow, client secrets, certificates, and employee-password UI. No matches.

ciEvidence.earlierFailedRunNote: The first run (commit c3c5ec5) failed 3 tests: missing Microsoft logging abstraction registration and SQLite connection-pool file locks in test cleanup. Both were fixed in the validated commit; the failed run is retained as history and is not used as evidence.

statusConclusion.completionLabel: Source Implementation Complete remains unearned (later milestones pending)

unexecutedChecks: interactive WPF startup on Windows Server 2019 (M7)
Microsoft Graph access and employee source resolution (M3)
limitations: M1 delivers structure and foundations only: interfaces for later phases have no implementations, and no fake or placeholder production services were registered.
The Windows CI run validates build, tests, and scans on a GitHub-hosted Windows runner; it does not prove real-tenant, interactive sign-in, transfer, or Windows Server 2019 behavior.
Local macOS checks were supplementary only; mandatory evidence comes from Windows CI on the exact validated commit.
The WPF smoke test constructs the window on Windows CI; full interactive startup on Windows Server 2019 remains an M7 production-acceptance check.
